intro: Priestleya humilis, commonly known as Indian spurge, is a plant species native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia, known for its medicinal properties and traditional use in folk medicine. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Priestleya humilis include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, Priestleya humilis is often prepared as a decoction or a powder, and is also used in combination with other herbs to make various herbal preparations, including tonics and ointments.
